title: The Cross Bronx Expressway is completed
book: The Power Broker: Robert Moses and the Fall of New York
author: Robert A. Caro
note: The Pulitzer Prize-winning biography of Robert Moses is the definitive account of how his highways, including the Cross Bronx Expressway, reshaped and scarred the city.

title: Demolition of the original Penn Station begins
book: The Late, Great Pennsylvania Station
author: Lorraine B. Diehl
note: The standard illustrated history of McKim, Mead & White's lost masterpiece and the demolition that launched the modern preservation movement.

title: The Puerto Rican Great Migration peaks
book: From Colonia to Community: The History of Puerto Ricans in New York City
author: Virginia Sánchez Korrol
note: The foundational scholarly history of the Puerto Rican community's growth in New York, tracing the migration that remade East Harlem and the Bronx.

title: The Ocean Hill-Brownsville teachers' strike
book: The Strike That Changed New York: Blacks, Whites, and the Ocean Hill-Brownsville Crisis
author: Jerald E. Podair
note: The authoritative account of the 1968 community-control fight that pitted Black self-determination against the teachers' union and reshaped city politics.

title: The Stonewall uprising ignites gay liberation
book: Stonewall: The Riots That Sparked the Gay Revolution
author: David Carter
note: The most thoroughly researched narrative history of the 1969 uprising, later the basis for the site's national-monument designation.

title: Hip-hop is born in the Bronx
book: Can't Stop Won't Stop: A History of the Hip-Hop Generation
author: Jeff Chang
note: The acclaimed, American Book Award-winning history of hip-hop's birth in the 1970s Bronx and its rise into a global culture.

title: 'Ford to City: Drop Dead'
book: Fear City: New York's Fiscal Crisis and the Rise of Austerity Politics
author: Kim Phillips-Fein
note: A Pulitzer finalist and the leading history of the 1975 fiscal crisis and the austerity politics it ushered in.

title: The blackout that broke the city's spirit
book: Ladies and Gentlemen, the Bronx Is Burning: 1977, Baseball, Politics, and the Battle for the Soul of a City
author: Jonathan Mahler
note: A vivid narrative history of New York in 1977 built around the July blackout, the Son of Sam summer and the Yankees.

title: The AIDS epidemic surfaces in New York
book: And the Band Played On: Politics, People, and the AIDS Epidemic
author: Randy Shilts
note: The landmark chronicle of the early AIDS crisis and the institutional failures that let it spread, with New York at its center.

title: ACT UP forms to fight the AIDS crisis
book: Let the Record Show: A Political History of ACT UP New York, 1987-1993
author: Sarah Schulman
note: The definitive, deeply reported oral and political history of ACT UP New York and its confrontational fight for AIDS treatment.

title: The Central Park jogger case
book: The Central Park Five: A Chronicle of a City Wilding
author: Sarah Burns
note: The authoritative account of the wrongful conviction of five Black and Latino teenagers and their eventual exoneration.

title: CompStat and the great crime decline
book: The City That Became Safe: New York's Lessons for Urban Crime and Its Control
author: Franklin E. Zimring
note: The leading empirical study of New York's historic crime decline and what actually drove it.

title: 9/11 attacks destroy the World Trade Center
book: The Looming Tower: Al-Qaeda and the Road to 9/11
author: Lawrence Wright
note: The Pulitzer Prize-winning history of al-Qaeda and the intelligence failures that led to the September 11 attacks.

title: 9/11 Memorial opens at Ground Zero
book: The Only Plane in the Sky: An Oral History of 9/11
author: Garrett M. Graff
note: The acclaimed oral history that assembles firsthand accounts of the attacks into a single narrative of the day.

title: Lehman Brothers collapse jolts Wall Street
book: Too Big to Fail: The Inside Story of How Wall Street and Washington Fought to Save the Financial System
author: Andrew Ross Sorkin
note: The definitive blow-by-blow account of the 2008 crisis and Lehman's collapse on Wall Street.

title: Occupy Wall Street takes Zuccotti Park
book: The Democracy Project: A History, a Crisis, a Movement
author: David Graeber
note: A firsthand history and analysis of Occupy Wall Street by the anthropologist credited with helping coin 'We are the 99%.'

title: Hurricane Sandy devastates the city
book: The Big Ones: How Natural Disasters Have Shaped Us (and What We Can Do About Them)
author: Lucy Jones
note: A leading seismologist's authoritative survey of major natural disasters, including Sandy, and how cities can prepare for them.

title: The Great Migration remakes Harlem
book: When Harlem Was in Vogue
author: David Levering Lewis
note: The standard narrative history of the Harlem Renaissance by the two-time Pulitzer-winning historian.

title: Immigration reform reopens the golden door
book: The Guarded Gate: Bigotry, Eugenics, and the Law That Kept Two Generations of Jews, Italians, and Other European Immigrants Out of America
author: Daniel Okrent
note: A widely praised history of the national-origins quota regime that the 1965 Hart-Celler Act finally dismantled.

title: UN chooses New York City for its headquarters
book: Capital of the World: The Race to Host the United Nations
author: Charlene Mires
note: The definitive history of the contest among American cities to host the UN and how New York won the headquarters.

title: Jackie Robinson breaks baseball's color barrier
book: Jackie Robinson: A Biography
author: Arnold Rampersad
note: The authoritative biography of Robinson, written with the estate's cooperation by a distinguished literary historian.

title: 'Saturday Night Live' debuts on NBC
book: Live from New York: The Complete, Uncensored History of Saturday Night Live
author: Tom Shales and James Andrew Miller
note: The definitive oral history of SNL from its 1975 Studio 8H debut onward.

title: CBGB opens on the Bowery
book: Please Kill Me: The Uncensored Oral History of Punk
author: Legs McNeil and Gillian McCain
note: The classic oral history of the New York punk scene that CBGB incubated, from the Ramones to Patti Smith.

title: New York City becomes COVID-19 epicenter
book: The Premonition: A Pandemic Story
author: Michael Lewis
note: A bestselling narrative of the public-health failures behind America's pandemic response as COVID-19 overwhelmed cities like New York.
